commit.message:
Fix false-positive auth detection and PM retry on PMSpec failure

- Add negative lookbehind in CliInteractiveWatchdog auth regex to exclude
  'No authentication required' from triggering fail-fast (was matching
  'authentication required' substring)
- Reset _pmSpecCreated flag on failure so PM retries on next loop iteration
  instead of permanently giving up

refactor: create per-project platform MI in Terraform instead of referencing shared mi-crossplane

- Replace data source azurerm_user_assigned_identity with resource so each
  project provisions its own isolated identity instead of sharing mi-crossplane
- Rename to mi-<project_name>-platform — reflects actual role: AKS cluster
  identity, kubelet identity, and Crossplane/ADO agent workload identity

  group, granting it KV Administrator + AKS RBAC Cluster Admin via group
  membership (requires sp-crossplane GroupMember.ReadWrite.All on Graph API)

  connection serviceprincipalid, eliminating crossplane_sp_client_id variable

commit.message:
Add therapy analysis and fix AID basal assessment bug

- New tools/report/therapy_analyzer.py: deep therapy settings analysis
  accounting for AID loop compensation. Uses per-night overnight drift
  instead of misleading metabolic flux, IOB circadian profiling,
  correction bolus ISF estimation, hypo safety analysis, weekly
  trend decomposition, and evidence-based recommendations.

- Fix assess_basal() AID bug: metabolic net_flux shortcut gave wrong
  answer for AID patients because flux includes loop adjustments
  (temp basals, SMBs), not just programmed basal. Overnight glucose
  slope is the reliable signal. Patient with flat overnight glucose
  (-0.03 mg/dL/hr) was incorrectly assessed as 'too_high' due to
  large negative net_flux from Loop's automated insulin delivery.

- Enhanced HTML report (generate_html_report.py): new Therapy tab with
  IOB circadian chart, weekly TIR stacked bars, overnight drift bars,
  hypo by hour histogram, dawn phenomenon analysis, and therapy
  recommendations panel with priority-colored cards.

- Added 4 tests for basal assessment including regression test for
  AID flux override bug (89 tests total, all passing).

Therapy findings for live-recent patient:
  - Basal: mixed (51% rising, 39% falling) → loop compensating
  - ISF: profile 40, effective 44 (1.10×) → reasonable
  - Only 11 manual boluses / 6 carb entries in 97 days
  - 1.0 hypo/day (17 serious) → safety concern
  - Weekly TIR range 25-90% → variable (external factors)
  - Dawn phenomenon: IOB 0.5U→2.1U (1-3AM to 4-7AM)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
chore: bump version to 0.0.57

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

docs: hypo supply-demand decomposition analysis (EXP-1601 to 1606)

Analyzes 1,661 hypo episodes across 11 AID patients through the
supply × demand metabolic framework. Key findings:

- The 'residual flip': residual swings from -2.14 (active descent)
  to +5.12 (early recovery) — a 7.26 mg/dL/step swing at nadir.
  The entire recovery is driven by unmodeled forces.

- 87% of recoveries have NO entered carbs (p=0.033 vs announced).
  Model is blind to the primary glucose-raising force.

- IOB at onset has zero correlation with rebound magnitude (r=-0.011).
  Rebound is determined by unmeasured rescue carb quantity.

- UVA/Padova-inspired decomposition reveals opposing errors in hypo
  range: hepatic model over-predicts (-2.2), while insulin effectiveness
  is over-predicted (+1.6). These cancel in aggregate but represent
  different fixable physics errors.

- Information ceiling: all R² values negative. Supply-demand framework
  reveals structure but can't improve prediction without rescue carb
  data (the dominant unmeasured input).

Includes analysis script, 6 experiment JSON results, 6 visualizations,
and research report with 16 assumptions flagged for expert review.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(research): EXP-1567 within-patient meal clock regularity analysis

Analyzes per-patient meal timing regularity using personal peak detection
on smoothed hourly histograms with ±1.5h (3h window) clustering.

Key findings:
- Population mean weighted std = 3.97h (range 1.00-7.29h)
- 2/11 patients (18%) are clock-like (std < 2h): g=1.00h, j=1.58h
- Lunch most synchronized across patients (inter-patient std 0.26h)
- Dinner most variable within-patient (mean std 1.01h)
- Weekend regularity: 50/50 split (no systematic effect)
- Individual entropy (0.841) < population entropy (0.938):
  personal meal clocks exist but differ per person

Includes:
- EXP-1567 experiment function with personal peak detection
- fig25: personal meal clock small multiples (11 patients)
- fig26: inter-patient variation heatmap
- fig27: weekday vs weekend regularity scatter
- Phase 6 report with tier classification and AID implications

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(regexp): host_fn_set_property for lastIndex non-writable errors

Temporarily hide try_stack when setting properties from host functions
to prevent handle_throw from corrupting VM state by finding try-catch
entries from caller JS code. Also add is_regexp_check and
host_fn_to_length helpers for upcoming fixes.

Fixes 5 tests: @@match/@@replace/@@search lastIndex non-writable.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: move API route tests out of src/pages/ to prevent Astro build failure

Astro treats all files in src/pages/ as page routes, so test files
containing vi.mock() were being executed during the build, causing
'Vitest mocker was not initialized' errors.

Moved 4 test files to src/__tests__/pages/ and updated their import
paths accordingly.

commit.message:
Remove @runInSeparateProcess to fix closure serialization error

PHPUnit's @runInSeparateProcess triggers global state serialization,
which fails because WordPress stores closures in global variables.
The send() method uses @header() (error-suppressed), so it works
fine without a separate process. Output is captured via ob_start().

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(research): EXP-1565 weekday vs weekend meal periodicity analysis

Test hypothesis that meal timing is bimodal (weekday vs weekend) with
different periodicity within each mode.

Key findings:
- Weekends are MORE temporally concentrated (entropy 0.925 vs 0.938)
  but LESS zone-aligned (62% vs 67% in canonical mealtime windows)
- Breakfast shifts +25 min on weekends; lunch/dinner are stable (±2min)
- Weekend meals are metabolically worse: +0.125 ISF-norm excursion (+8%)
- Per-day meal rate is remarkably constant (375 vs 372 meals/day)
- DOW distribution nearly uniform (365-384 meals per day-of-week)

New: _weekday_weekend_periodicity(), _per_patient_dow_analysis() helpers,

commit.message:
Add EP download progress bar to all samples (#621)

All samples now show per-EP download progress with name and percentage,
matching the pattern already in native-chat-completions samples.

**Changes (38 files across 4 languages):**
- **C# (9 samples)**: Replaced bare \DownloadAndRegisterEpsAsync()\ and
\RunWithSpinner\ calls with progress callback
- **JS/TS (12 samples)**: Replaced bare \downloadAndRegisterEps()\ with
progress callback
- **Python (9 samples)**: Replaced bare \download_and_register_eps()\
with progress callback
- **Rust (8 samples)**: Replaced \download_and_register_eps(None)\ with
\download_and_register_eps_with_progress\

Each callback tracks the current EP name and displays a carriage-return
progress line showing EP name (left-aligned 30 chars) and download
percentage.

commit.message:
feat(research): EXP-1563 multi-config metabolic characterization with periodicity

Compare ISF-normalized excursion, spectral power, and meal periodicity
across 3 detection configs (5g/30m, 5g/90m, 18g/90m).

Key findings:
- Small meals (5-18g) have 6.2x lower spectral power than large meals
- Therapy config (18g/90m) maximizes metabolic SNR by 27% over census
- Stricter detection modestly increases periodicity (entropy 0.946→0.938,
  zone fraction 61.0→65.6%) as snacks are preferentially removed
- Clustering gap affects spectral power non-monotonically: longer gaps
  decrease per-hour density despite merging adjacent meals

New: shared helpers (_collect_meal_metabolic_records, _metabolic_by_carb_range,
_mealtime_periodicity), 6 visualization figures (fig15-fig20), Phase 4 report.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
r600: harmonized VLIW5 scheduler architecture — 7 inviolable laws

Definitive master architecture document for the C2-C6 scheduler track,
synthesized from Lima PP analysis, Itanium IA-64 patterns, hostile
reviewer mandates, and hardware probe data.

7 Physical Laws of TeraScale-2 VLIW5 Scheduling:
1. PV/PS ephemeral bypass (1-cycle inter-bundle lifespan)
2. Clause continuity outweighs Sarkar register pressure
3. KCACHE clause-lock limit (max 2 lines per CF_ALU)
4. GPR bank collisions (4 SRAM banks, no dispersal network)
5. T-slot literal monopoly (1 literal locks transcendental unit)
6. WAR edge co-issue legal (same-bundle read+write safe)
7. Ban modulo scheduling (no hardware register rotation)

Includes: 5-step C4 constraint cascade, composite C5 priority function,
terakan_resource_state struct definition, cross-architecture pattern
source table, and C-track phase dependency DAG.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
r600: add Lima PP (Mali-400) reference corpus and analysis

14 source files from Mesa src/gallium/drivers/lima/ir/pp/ covering the
full Mali-400 pixel pipeline compiler: scheduler, packer, register
allocator, codegen, liveness, and compaction passes.

Includes 1176-line LIMA_PP_SCHEDULER_ANALYSIS.md mapping all 10 Lima
patterns to TeraScale: Sarkar reg_pressure formula, 11-constraint
cascade, source reordering for bypass eligibility, constant dedup.

Critical finding: Lima MUL→ADD bypass is intra-instruction (same VLIW
word) while TeraScale PV/PS is inter-bundle (group N→N+1), proving
C4/C5 must be interleaved, not sequential passes.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
r600: add Itanium IA-64 research corpus for VLIW scheduler study

10 PDFs (IA-64 ISA volumes, compiler optimization guides, MIT VLIW
compilation paper, microarchitecture optimization, Open64 reference)
plus 7 text extracts covering bundle templates, dispersal rules,
latency tables, and scheduling heuristics.

These reference materials inform the C2-C6 scheduler track for the
TeraScale-2 VLIW5 compiler. Key patterns extracted: stop bit placement,
reservation tables, critical-path-height priority, and the proof that
Itanium register rotation (modulo scheduling) has no TeraScale analog.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add report suite: summary, validation vignettes, private live-data report

Reports:
- autoproductionize-summary-report: 6 production modules with evidence
- clinical-inference-vignettes: 11-patient validation with per-patient analysis
- private live-recent report (git-ignored, in externals/)

Visualizations:
- 7 summary figures (fidelity, harmonic, ISF, alerts, confidence, meals, overview)
- 5 validation figures (dashboard, circadian grid, meals, fidelity, recs)
- 2 private figures (glucose overview, clinical summary)

Bug fixes:
- Fix pandas Index propagation in metabolic_engine (net_flux, hours)
  Caused fidelity assessment to fail for all patients
- Ensure _extract_hours returns numpy array, not pandas Index
- Fix DetectedMeal.is_announced -> .announced field name

Fidelity results: 2 good (j,k), 2 acceptable (b,d), 7 poor
ADA/Fidelity concordance: validates 36% research finding

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(research): EXP-1561 meal response metabolic characterization

Add ISF-normalized excursion and supply×demand spectral power analysis
by carb range, extending the Phase 1-2 natural experiments work:

Key findings:
- ISF normalization re-ranks patients: f (ISF=21) → 5.0 correction-equiv
  vs b (ISF=95) → 0.77, revealing true metabolic cost
- Spectral power scales 21× from 10-19g to ≥50g (super-linear)
- ISF-norm excursion and spectral power are ORTHOGONAL (r=-0.039) —
  independent dimensions capturing glycemic impact vs AID activity
- Carbs predict spectral power better (r=0.433) than excursion (r=0.149)
- Handles mmol/L ISF auto-detection via _extract_isf_scalar()

New artifacts:
- EXP-1561 experiment in exp_clinical_1551.py
- Phase 3 report: natural-experiments-metabolic-report-2026-04-09.md
- fig9: three-panel carb range (raw vs ISF-norm vs spectral)
- fig10: per-patient ISF normalization effect
- fig11: cross-metric scatter correlations

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: print/PDF support — each section fits one A4 landscape page

- @page { size: A4 landscape; margin: 0 }
- Each section: page-break-after: always, min/max-height: 210mm
- Force background color printing (-webkit-print-color-adjust: exact)
- Hide nav, scroll indicators in print
- Prevent breaks inside cards and tables

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: enforce non-boundary task scores across grader and inference output

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix podman staticfiles issues on WSL and add WhiteNoise

- Fixed PermissionError in collectstatic by separating staticfiles and media
  into Docker volumes instead of bind mounts (resolves WSL permission issues)
- Changed network mode from 'slirp4netns' to 'host' to avoid Pasta/IPv6 errors
  on AlmaLinux WSL
- Updated DB_HOST to 'localhost' in .env.container for host network mode
- Added whitenoise package to requirements.txt and configured middleware
- Updated cerebro_inception.sh to force slirp4netns backend for podman build
- Improved DB restoration logic in inception script to handle constraint errors
- Renamed image from 'sherpa_web' to 'cerebro_web' for consistency

Static files now collect and serve correctly (347 files collected).
Container network connectivity working on AlmaLinux WSL with Podman rootless.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
🐛 Corrige 5 bugs: box ovo/caixa, activeIndex, contagem Box, busca retroativa

- CSS: adiciona max-height + overflow-y ao #groupParticipantsList
  (lista de participantes do grupo sem scroll com >3 jogadores)

- hatchEggFromInventory: usa addToSharedBox() ao invés de player.box.push()
  quando o time está cheio; monstrinhos de ovo não ficam mais invisíveis na UI

- hatchEggFromInventory: corrige boxSize para contar de sharedBox filtrada
  por jogador ao invés do legado player.box (limite MAX_TOTAL correto)

- moveTeamToBox: atualiza player.activeIndex após splice para evitar que
  o índice aponte para o monstrinho errado após enviar para a caixa

- Cartão de jogador: exibe contagem de Box a partir de sharedBox (correto)
  ao invés de player.box (array legado, sempre vazio)

- applyKitSwapRetroactive: busca instanceId também na sharedBox,
  não apenas em player.box (que nunca tem monstrinhos no sistema atual)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: projetogg <256972595+projetogg@users.noreply.github.com>
